articles.title: Pentagon to Order 30,000 One-Way Drones in 'Next Few Days'
articles.excerpt: The DoD announced selection of winners from its Gauntlet unmanned exercise competition, with plans to rapidly procure 30,000 one-way attack drones for deployment in contested environments.

articles.title: Army Launches Project ARIA to Accelerate AI Adoption
articles.excerpt: The U.S. Army has partnered with technology firms to develop AI capabilities for military operations under the new Project ARIA initiative, targeting faster integration of artificial intelligence across service branches.

articles.title: SBIR/STTR Programs Reauthorized After Five-Month Funding Freeze
articles.excerpt: Senators Ernst and Markey reached a bipartisan deal to reauthorize SBIR and STTR programs for five years, ending a freeze that halted new solicitations across 11 federal agencies. The compromise establishes a new Strategic Breakthrough Initiative to help small companies transition from development to production.

articles.title: Pentagon's New Chief Data Officer to Push 'AI Capabilities to Warfighters'
articles.excerpt: The Pentagon has filled key data and cybersecurity leadership positions to accelerate AI integration for military operations, with the new CDO focused on delivering actionable AI tools to frontline forces.

articles.title: DIU Offers $100M in Prizes for Voice-Controlled AI Drone Swarm Orchestrator
articles.excerpt: The Defense Innovation Unit is offering up to $100 million for companies developing software that allows a single servicemember to command entire formations of unmanned vehicles through plain English voice or text commands.

articles.title: DIU, Navy Seek Long-Range Drones for Maritime Strikes
articles.excerpt: The Defense Innovation Unit issued a solicitation for unmanned systems capable of carrying 1,000-pound munitions with a 600-nautical-mile combat radius, designed to operate from ships with minimal infrastructure including destroyers.

articles.title: Missile Makers Agree to 'Quadruple' Production, Trump Says
articles.excerpt: Defense manufacturers pledged to dramatically increase weapons output following sustained White House pressure to accelerate production timelines for advanced munitions systems.

articles.title: Istari Wins DoW OTA for Mission Engineering Automation Testbed
articles.excerpt: The Department of War awarded Istari a prototype Other Transaction Authority agreement for the Mission Engineering Automation Testbed (MEAT) platform, advancing digital engineering capabilities for defense acquisition.

articles.title: DOE Funds 10 Quantum Computing Projects Under QC3 Program
articles.excerpt: ARPA-E awarded $37 million to quantum computing initiatives including Boeing and PsiQuantum, advancing quantum capabilities with potential defense and national security applications.

articles.title: The Pentagon Moves Into the Era of Affordable Mass
articles.excerpt: Military leaders are exploring cost-effective mass production strategies to counter enemy drone swarms that are rapidly depleting expensive air defense interceptors, signaling a strategic shift toward attritable systems.

articles.title: Trump's New Cyber Strategy Calls for Tougher Responses to Threats
articles.excerpt: The administration released a condensed cybersecurity strategy emphasizing more aggressive counteroffensive measures against adversary cyber operations, marking a significant shift from prior defensive postures.

articles.title: Germany Reaches for European Conventional Military Dominance
articles.excerpt: Germany is accelerating defense spending to 150 billion euros annually by 2029, reversing decades of underinvestment and reshaping the European defense industrial base with implications for transatlantic partnerships.

articles.title: Navy Seeking Industry Partner to Manage SLAM2ER Technology Consortium
articles.excerpt: Naval Surface Warfare Center Panama City Division issued an RFI for a consortium management firm to oversee the SLAM2ER advanced weapons technology consortium, expanding industry collaboration on naval strike capabilities.

articles.title: AI 'Fundamentally Changing' Adversary Behavior, Leading to Force Generation Reforms
articles.excerpt: Pentagon officials acknowledged that artificial intelligence is reshaping how adversaries operate on the battlefield, requiring revised force structure planning and accelerated adoption of AI-enabled defense capabilities.
